Title: South Sudan raid: 4,000 cattle stolen, 3 killed
Summary: A raid in Warrap State, South Sudan, resulted in three deaths and the theft of over 4,000 cattle. Authorities from Warrap State suspect armed youth from Unity State were responsible, though Unity State officials dispute this, suggesting the attackers were from within Gogrial East County. Security forces have been deployed to the affected areas of Alabek and Lietnhom.
Keywords: South Sudan, Warrap State, cattle raiding, Gogrial East County, Unity State

Title: Brother Killed in Aweil East Family Dispute
Summary: A 26-year-old man in Aweil East County, South Sudan, killed his 30-year-old elder brother during a family dispute. The incident occurred after the elder brother, reportedly intoxicated, began threatening family members. The younger brother intervened, leading to a physical confrontation that resulted in the elder brother's death from injuries, possibly to the lung and heart.
Keywords: Aweil East, family dispute, homicide, intoxication, South Sudan

Title: Yuai on Edge as Rival Forces Build Up in Jonglei
Summary: Tensions are high in Yuai, Jonglei State, as SSPDF and SPLA-IO forces prepare for a showdown following recent fighting that saw Yuai fall to SPLA-IO. Civilians have fled the town, creating a dire humanitarian situation with over 10,000 internally displaced persons facing hunger. Both sides are reinforcing positions, with SPLA-IO asserting firm control over Uror and Nyirol while denying allegations of seizing NGO vehicles.
Keywords: Yuai, Jonglei, SSPDF, SPLA-IO, South Sudan conflict, humanitarian crisis

Title: Oyet Rejects Resignation Talk, Signals Unity Push
Summary: Gen. Nathaniel Oyet, Acting Chairman of SPLM/A-IO, has publicly denied claims of his resignation, reaffirming his commitment to the movement. His statement addresses a circulating resignation letter he deems inauthentic and questions the legitimacy of a note attributed to Dr. Riek Machar regarding his role. Oyet emphasized the constitutional basis of his position and the interconnectedness of the movement's political and military wings, calling for unity amidst internal disputes.
Keywords: Nathaniel Oyet, SPLM/A-IO, Riek Machar, resignation, unity, South Sudan

Title: South Sudan’s Oil Dilemma in Sudan War Spillover
Summary: South Sudan is increasingly entangled in Sudan's civil war, facing accusations of supporting the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) while navigating its own domestic power struggles. The conflict, particularly around the strategic Heglig oil field, highlights South Sudan's economic reliance on oil pipelines through Sudan and the dire humanitarian consequences for civilians.
Keywords: South Sudan, Sudan War, Oil, RSF, SAF, Heglig

Title: Deadly Aweng Land Clash: Chiefs Suspended
Summary: A land dispute in Aweng Payam, Twic County, Warrap State, escalated into violence, resulting in two deaths. Three traditional chiefs have been suspended due to allegations of incitement, and an investigation is underway with several arrests made. Interim leadership has been appointed to ensure peace and justice, highlighting the ongoing challenge of land disputes in South Sudan.
Keywords: land dispute, Twic County, Aweng Payam, chiefs suspended

Title: Cross-Border Peace Talks Stall Over Cattle Routes
Summary: A cross-border peace conference in South Sudan was postponed due to a disagreement between the Misseriya and Mayom communities over cattle migration routes. The conference, aimed at reducing tensions and addressing seasonal movements, involved several communities and was funded by UNMISS and the South Sudan government. Mayom County declined to participate at the last minute, awaiting further engagement from Misseriya leaders.
Keywords: South Sudan, Misseriya, Mayom, Cattle Migration, Peace Talks, Abiemnhom

Title: Peace Envoy Chief Dies in Yuai Crossfire Shock
Summary: Moses Machot Gierkuach, a community head chief from Duk County on a peace mission, was killed in Yuai town, Uror County, South Sudan, during clashes between the SSPDF and SPLA-IO. His death, possibly from crossfire or assassination, is a significant loss for local peacemaking efforts. Insecurity prevented his body from being returned home, highlighting the region's volatile conditions.
Keywords: South Sudan, Jonglei State, peace mission, crossfire, community chief, SPLA-IO, SSPDF, Yuai

Title: Rumbek Conference Sparks Hopes of Lasting Peace
Summary: Community leaders from the Agar community opened a five-day conference in Rumbek, Lakes State, South Sudan, aiming to address years of inter-communal conflict. The gathering, initiated by the Rumbek Youth Union, brings together diverse participants to draft proposals for reconciliation, focusing on issues like gun violence, land disputes, and youth rivalries. Discussions also include the role of traditional mechanisms, bride wealth, and the potential for digital platforms to sustain dialogue and peace efforts.
Keywords: Rumbek Conference, Agar community, South Sudan, inter-communal peace, reconciliation

Title: Unity State on Edge: Commanders Shape New Frontlines
Summary: Unity State in South Sudan has become the epicenter of new hostilities, driven by localized command structures and personal networks rather than directives from Juba. The conflict involves government forces, primarily Bul Nuer, and opposition units tied to Leek, Jikany, Dok, and Nyuong communities, with control over key areas like the Bentiu-Heglig road crucial for oil revenues and resupply. Persistent flooding, oil pollution, and the closure of Sudan's border exacerbate the humanitarian crisis, while community leaders express frustration over the lack of implementation of peace agreements on the ground.
Keywords: Unity State, South Sudan, Civil War, Bentiu, Oil Revenues, Humanitarian Crisis
Location: Unity State; Bentiu; Rubkona; Guit; Juba; Heglig; Leer; Nairobi

Title: South Sudan Forces Urged to Heed War Laws Now
Summary: Renewed clashes between South Sudan's armed forces in Jonglei and beyond are raising concerns about adherence to war laws. Observers and activists emphasize the importance of international humanitarian law, particularly regarding the protection of civilians and the prevention of sexual violence. There's a call for accountability, including through a potential Hybrid Court, and for mandatory training in humanitarian law to reduce violations and protect communities.
Keywords: South Sudan, War Laws, Humanitarian Law, Jonglei, Accountability

Title: Pardoned Rebel Leader Flown to Juba in Secret Deal
Summary: Rebel leader Leek Mamer Leek of the Red Belt group was pardoned and airlifted to Juba as part of a secret deal aimed at reducing communal friction in South Sudan. This initiative seeks to co-opt grassroots fighters into community policing units rather than engaging them militarily. The agreement involved nullifying rebellion charges and integrating Red Belt members, reflecting a broader strategy by Juba to manage local armed groups through strategic co-option.
Keywords: South Sudan, Red Belt, Juba, Pardon, Rebel Leader, Security Deal

Title: Fresh Clashes Shake South Sudan Peace Hopes
Summary: Recent clashes between the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces and SPLM/A-IO units across Jonglei, Unity, and Eastern Equatoria threaten the Revitalized Agreement and imperil peace hopes. Humanitarian efforts are being hampered by fighting, and there are calls for renewed commitment to the ceasefire and political negotiations. International bodies and neighboring countries are urging restraint and mediation to prevent further escalation.
Keywords: South Sudan, conflict, peace agreement, humanitarian crisis, mediation

Title: Fighting Surges as South Sudan Eyes 2026 Polls
Summary: Fighting has intensified in South Sudan between the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces and the SPLA-IO, further eroding the 2018 peace accord. Civilian populations are suffering greatly, facing violence, displacement, and a lack of humanitarian aid. Activists like Edmund Yakani are condemning leaders and urging youth to reject recruitment, warning that continued conflict could lead to ethnically driven atrocities and undermine the upcoming 2026 elections.
Keywords: South Sudan, conflict, peace accord, 2026 polls, humanitarian crisis
Location: South Sudan; Unity State; Nasir; Greater Equatoria; Upper Nile

Title: Sudden Shift: Bentiu Camp Falls to Government Forces
Summary: Government forces in South Sudan seized the Bentiu IDP camp after a brief battle with opposition fighters, marking a significant breakdown of the 2018 security arrangements. The takeover, intended to restore law and order, is seen by opposition as a violation of ceasefire clauses, raising concerns about future stability in the region.
Keywords: Bentiu, South Sudan, SPLA-IO, SSPDF, IDP camp, conflict

Title: Deadly Warrap Showdown Leaves 14 Dead, 15 Injured
Summary: Clashes between the Nyarmuong and Jur-Man-Anger communities in Warrap State have resulted in 14 deaths and 15 injuries, fueled by revenge killings and armed youth mobilization. The violence, sparked by a shooting, is complicated by difficult terrain and limited access for peacekeepers, prompting calls for reconciliation and peace forums.
Keywords: Warrap State, community clashes, revenge killings, South Sudan

Title: SPLA-IO Threatens New Strikes, Civilians Alerted
Summary: The SPLA-IO has announced an "active defence" campaign against government forces in Juba, citing the need to alleviate citizen suffering. They have warned civilians to avoid military installations and appealed to government soldiers to defect, while also requesting NGOs and UN agencies to coordinate movements to ensure humanitarian access.
Keywords: SPLA-IO, South Sudan, Juba, conflict, humanitarian

Title: Artillery Thunder Jolts Bentiu, Tensions Soar
Summary: Heavy artillery shelling hit opposition-held areas near Bentiu, Unity State, escalating tensions and raising fears of catastrophic displacement due to ongoing flooding. The flare-up follows border skirmishes and highlights the fragility of South Sudan's 2018 peace accord, with aid workers warning of severe humanitarian consequences. Diplomatic efforts are underway to secure a ceasefire and humanitarian corridors.
Keywords: Bentiu, Unity State, South Sudan, Artillery, Conflict, Displacement, Peace Accord

Title: 1,000 SSOA Fighters Switch Sides in Jonglei Shock
Summary: Over a thousand fighters from the South Sudan Opposition Alliance (SSOA) in Jonglei State have switched allegiance to the Sudan People’s Liberation Army–In Opposition (SPLA-IO) after a rally. Brigadier General Much Dak Banang, an SSOA commander, rejected integrating forces into the national army, citing incomplete peace accord implementation and civilian burden. This defection highlights the unstable loyalties and escalating tensions in Jonglei, despite the 2018 peace agreement.
Keywords: South Sudan, Jonglei, SPLA-IO, SSOA, Peace Accord

Title: SPLA-IO Seizes Key Border Garrison in Unity State
Summary: Renewed hostilities erupted in Nyuelnyuel, a strategic border settlement in Unity State, South Sudan, between government troops and SPLA-IO fighters. The clash, which saw SPLA-IO pushing back government forces, has caused civilian displacement and raised concerns about the fragile 2018 peace agreement and its security arrangements.
Keywords: SPLA-IO, Unity State, South Sudan, peace agreement

Title: Cueibet Tension: Five Killed in Lakes State Clash
Summary: Five people were killed and dozens wounded in clashes in Cueibet County, Lakes State, South Sudan, stemming from a disputed livestock raid. The fighting occurred despite the presence of reconciliation leaders and highlights concerns about weak local governance and a power vacuum. Community leaders are calling for sustained dialogue and a permanent county commissioner to address the entrenched hostility.
Keywords: Cueibet County, Lakes State, cattle raiding, South Sudan conflict, local governance

Title: Red Belt Leader: Nairobi Plot or Self-Defense?
Summary: Leek Mamer Leek, leader of the Red Belt militia in South Sudan, claims a 2025 visit to Nairobi led to false accusations of rebellion, stemming from his refusal to join opposition figures. The government officially declared his group an insurgent movement, despite his insistence on community protection duties. This situation highlights the ambiguous line between community defense and insurgency in South Sudan, leaving civilians in Jonglei hoping for clarity.
Keywords: Red Belt, Leek Mamer Leek, South Sudan, Jonglei, insurgency, community militia

Title: Yuai and Nadapal Rebel Advances Shock South Sudan
Summary: Rebel forces, SPLA-IO, have reportedly overrun South Sudan People’s Defence Forces positions in Yuai and Nadapal. These advances are strategically significant, impacting trade, security, and humanitarian efforts. The renewed conflict raises concerns about the 2018 peace deal and potential regional instability.
Keywords: South Sudan, SPLA-IO, Nadapal, Yuai, conflict, peace deal

Title: Bishop’s Bold Call: Ballots or Bullets for 2026?
Summary: Bishop William of South Sudan urged for peaceful elections in 2026, emphasizing unity and inclusivity to prevent a return to conflict. He called for a national day of prayer and cautioned against the manipulation of unemployed youth by politicians, highlighting the critical role of the church in national reconciliation and stability.
Keywords: South Sudan, elections, peace, church, conflict
